title: FRONDED LANDED at 33aeec40 — Pelagic go-signal + tooling-friction note for Pelagic per owner direction
body:
From the 7cf730 coordinator session. LANDED: 33aeec40 feat(agent-tools): improve collaboration cli ergonomics. Owner-directed coordinator landing of Fronded Climbing Pollen 686bfd's collaboration-cli-gaps bundle per Fronded's session-complete handoff event d03101a9. 17 files / 933 insertions / 70 deletions; all hooks passed (74 turbo tasks, 23 cached, 30s). Commit body credits Fronded Climbing Pollen as primary author + Asteroid 0c263b for the absorbed cross-lane repairs. CLAIMS CLEARED: Fronded's git:index/head claim 686bfd14 closed; files-area claim 686bfd00 closed. Active-claims registry now EMPTY. Index/head is fully open for the next agent. GO-SIGNAL TO PELAGIC SWIMMING RUDDER 9a249c: per the coordinator sequence, you are next. Your identity-wordlist refactor + supporting state (wordlists.ts split into wordlists-aerial / -botanical / -celestial / -ember / -maritime / -nocturnal + agent-identity.md docs + derive.unit.test.ts updates) can land now. CRITICAL ASK from owner direction 13:05Z: please ensure your refactor preserves seed-to-name stability for in-flight session prefixes (or includes an env-file migration so existing Claude/Cursor sessions retain their resolved names). The 7cf730 -> Twilit -> Ashen drift in this session and the wordlist-induced friction observed across multiple agents today are the diagnostic. Coordinator landing path is available if you would prefer a handoff (mirroring Fronded's pattern), or you may run your own commit window. TOOLING-FRICTION NOTE FOR PELAGIC per your standing direction (event 9a249c-tooling-feedback-standing-note + 2dbd74f6) and owner aside 13:24Z about Python access patterns: Surface: agent-tools collaboration-state and commit-queue CLIs. Workflow: agents need to read individual comms-events, find claims by agent/prefix, identify queue entries by agent or status. Observed: throughout today's coordinator role I had to fall back to inline Python (python3 -c 'import json; ...') to: - List comms-events newer than a timestamp filtered by author or audience - Read individual comms-event bodies (no comms show or comms read command) - Find claims by session_id_prefix (no claims list --filter --by-prefix) - Find queue entries by agent or status (no commit-queue list --filter) - Pretty-print active-claims state for situational awareness Expected: structured CLI affordances for these reads. Owner aside surfaced this directly: 'I notice you are using Python to access the logs... if this indicates a lacking agent tooling tool, please make a note'. Belongs in: agent-tools backlog. Suggested commands: - collaboration-state comms list [--since ] [--audience ] [--from ] - collaboration-state comms show - collaboration-state claims list [--prefix

] [--name ] [--thread ] [--kind files|git|workspace|...] - collaboration-state claims show - commit-queue list [--prefix

] [--phase ] - commit-queue show Other tooling frictions observed today (carry from earlier coordinator events): - claims close flag is --summary not --closure-summary (discoverability) - claims open --area-pattern vs --file (singular) ambiguity - comms render is fragile: chokes on a single malformed event JSON, blocking shared-comms-log.md regeneration; would benefit from --skip-malformed or per-file recovery - Identity drift mid-session under wordlist refactor (already captured in 59feb7e5 disclosure) Coordinator status: Fronded done; Pelagic next; Riverine and Forest done; Asteroid done. After Pelagic lands, coordination is complete and I'll close my own session.
